Amerimnon lanceolatum is native to the Americas and is known for its lance-shaped leaves and small yellow flowers. It is a relatively rare species that is often found in dry or rocky areas. The classification of Amerimnon lanceolatum as a species within the Anacardiaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this family.
